_description: Model derivations for Canada vs Morocco 2026-07-04 R16

rule_checks.RULE7: borderline INACTIVE -- Canada win=17.39%, just above the 15% threshold
rule_checks.RULE8: FIRES -- Draw=28.78% >= 25%. Comparison-type estimates shaded toward 0.50.
rule_checks.RULE15: borderline -- Canada win 17.39% is above the 10% Scenario-A floor, so no blanket underdog-forward suppression applied to Jonathan David. Davies' suppression is driven by his OWN literal non-participation data, not team-context blowout logic.
rule_checks.TEAM_SOT_EMPIRICAL_FLOOR (new, from JULY: Canada's SOT market-fit lambda (3.025) is drastically below their own 4-game empirical average (7.0, with a floor of 4 in every single game including a loss and a tense R32 win). This is the exact pattern flagged in the postmortem as costing -35.67 RBP across 4 recent matches when the market fit was trusted over empirical data. Applied here: blended 30% market / 70% empirical -> 0.68, a much higher number than the raw market-fit (0.358) would suggest.
rule_checks.PERSONAL_ZERO_OVERRIDES_TEAM_CONTEXT (ne: Applied to both Davies (0 shots across 4 apps, didn't play at all in 3 of 4 games) and Brahim Díaz (1 SOT in 4 games despite starting every game) -- both discounted well below their market prices (Davies: market 0.274 -> priced 0.12; Díaz: market 0.545 -> priced 0.33) rather than trusting the more generous illiquid/liquid market reads.

espn_insight.canada_sot_floor: 4, 10, 7, 7 -- never below 4 in any of 4 games this tournament, including a loss to Switzerland and a tense 1-0 R32 win. Strong, durable signal independent of opponent quality or game state.
espn_insight.jonathan_david_goal_concentration: All 3 group goals came in a single extreme blowout (Qatar, who had 2 red cards). Zero goals in the other 3 competitive games.
espn_insight.hakimi_involvement_concentration: Both goal involvements (1G, 1A) came in the same Haiti blowout game. Zero in BRA/SCO/NED.
espn_insight.saibari_ned_precedent: Blanked (0 shots) against Netherlands, his toughest opponent to date -- but Canada (17.4% win prob) is a considerably weaker opponent than Netherlands were for Morocco, supporting a reversion toward his earlier group-stage scoring form rather than the NED blank.
